title: Air Fryer Chicken Recipe with Vegetables
metaDescription: Air fryer chicken and vegetables with 38g of protein per serving — juicy chicken breast and roasted veggies in one basket, ready in 25 minutes.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: air fryer chicken, air fryer chicken and vegetables, high protein dinner
ingredients: 1 cup baby carrots or bell pepper strips

steps.name: Season everything
steps.text: Toss chicken breasts and vegetables separately with olive oil, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.

steps.name: Arrange in the air fryer
steps.text: Place chicken breasts in the air fryer basket in a single layer, with vegetables arranged around or on a separate rack if your air fryer has one.

steps.name: Air fry
steps.text: Cook at 380°F for 12-15 minutes, flipping the chicken halfway through, until internal temperature reaches 165°F. Add vegetables partway through if cooking in the same basket to avoid overcooking.

steps.name: Rest and serve
steps.text: Let the chicken rest 5 minutes before slicing. Plate with the vegetables and top with fresh parsley.

tips.question: Why is my air fryer chicken breast dry?
tips.answer: Overcooking past 165°F internal temperature is the most common cause — use a meat thermometer instead of relying on time alone, since air fryer wattage varies between models.

tips.question: Can I cook the chicken and vegetables together in one basket?
tips.answer: Yes, if your basket is large enough for a single layer of each without overcrowding — overcrowding blocks airflow and leads to steaming instead of crisping.

tips.question: What other vegetables work well in this recipe?
tips.answer: Zucchini, asparagus, and cauliflower all air fry well alongside chicken — just cut them to a similar size as the broccoli florets for even cooking.

tips.question: Do I need to preheat the air fryer?
tips.answer: A 3-minute preheat at 380°F helps the chicken get a better sear, though most air fryers will still cook it through without preheating — just add 1-2 minutes to the cook time.

images.heroAlt: Sliced air fryer chicken breast with roasted broccoli and carrots on a white plate
images.textureAlt: Knife slicing through a juicy air fryer chicken breast revealing the moist interior
images.pinAlt: Fork lifting a slice of juicy air fryer chicken breast off a plate of roasted vegetables

storyIntro: The air fryer earns its keep on nights when a whole oven feels like too much commitment. Chicken breast and vegetables go in the same basket and come out in under 15 minutes of actual cook time.
38g of protein per serving, minimal cleanup, and none of the guesswork that usually comes with getting chicken breast to stay juicy.
storyNote: A meat thermometer solves the single biggest problem with air fryer chicken: wattage varies enough between models that going by time alone is how you end up overcooking it past 165°F.
